moves.name: Swear an Iron Vow
moves.triggerShort: When you swear to fulfill a quest.
moves.trigger: When you swear to fulfill a quest, write your vow and give the vow a rank. Then roll +heart. If your vow is to a person or community with whom you have a bond, add +1.
moves.strong: You are emboldened and begin your quest with confidence. Take +2 momentum.
moves.weak: You are determined but burdened by the nature of your quest. Take +1 momentum.
moves.miss:
You face a significant obstacle before you can begin your quest. Envision what stands in your way (Ask the Oracle if unsure), and choose one:
  • You press on: Suffer −2 momentum, and do what you must to overcome this obstacle.
  • You give up: Forsake Your Vow.
moves.notes: The vow must be meaningful — something you'd risk your life for. The rank (Troublesome through Epic) determines the difficulty of the progress track. The iron is literal in Ironsworn — swear on iron you carry. Vows drive the game.

moves.name: Reach a Milestone
moves.triggerShort: When you make significant progress toward a vow.
moves.trigger: When you make significant progress toward a vow by overcoming a critical obstacle, completing a perilous expedition, solving a complex mystery, defeating a powerful threat, or gaining a critical resource, mark progress on your vow.
moves.notes: No roll — just mark progress when the fiction clearly warrants it. A milestone is a meaningful step: defeating a key obstacle, uncovering a vital secret, completing a significant sub-goal. Don't mark milestones for minor victories.

moves.name: Fulfill Your Vow
moves.triggerShort: When you believe you have fulfilled your vow.
moves.trigger: When you believe you have fulfilled your vow, roll the challenge dice against your progress track. This is a progress roll — do not add a stat. Momentum is ignored.
moves.strong: Your quest is complete. Mark experience equal to the vow's rank (Troublesome: 1, Dangerous: 2, Formidable: 3, Extreme: 4, Epic: 5).
moves.weak: There is more to be done, or you realize the truth of your quest. Envision what you discover (Ask the Oracle if unsure). Mark experience at one rank lower (Troublesome: 0, Dangerous: 1, Formidable: 2, Extreme: 3, Epic: 4). You may Swear an Iron Vow to set things right. If you do, add +1.
moves.miss:
Your quest is undone. Envision what happens (Ask the Oracle if unsure), and choose one:
  • You recommit: Clear all but one filled progress, and raise the quest's rank by one (if not already epic).
  • You give up: Forsake Your Vow.
moves.notes: Progress roll against the vow's track. No action die. Momentum is ignored. Use the progress spinner to enter your vow's current progress score (0–10).

moves.name: Forsake Your Vow
moves.triggerShort: When you renounce your quest or are unable to continue.
moves.trigger: When you renounce your quest or are unable to continue, clear the vow and Endure Stress equal to the vow's rank (Troublesome: 1, Dangerous: 2, Formidable: 3, Extreme: 4, Epic: 5).
moves.notes: No roll — just the consequence. Use it when a vow becomes impossible or when the character deliberately walks away from their oath. Breaking an iron vow is a serious act that costs spirit proportional to the gravity of the abandoned quest.

moves.name: Advance
moves.triggerShort: When you spend experience to gain or upgrade an asset.
moves.trigger: When you focus on your skills, receive training, find inspiration, or earn recognition, and you have experience to spend, you may spend 3 experience to add a new asset, or 2 experience to upgrade an existing asset.
moves.notes: No roll. Spend experience earned from Fulfill Your Vow. Assets are permanent abilities that define your character's specialties. Choose assets that fit your concept — interesting is better than optimal.
